results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE PURPOSE OF THE PROJECT IS TO MEASURE THE RELEASE OF MICROPLASTICS FROM SYNTHETIC TEXTILES DURING WASHING AND EXAMINE THE NANOSCALE DEGRADATION MECHANISMS UNDER SIMULATED ENVIRONMENTAL EXPOSURE THAT MICROPLASTICS ENCOUNTER UPON RELEASE TO NATURAL WATER BODIES.ACTIVITIES:THE SPECIFIC OBJECTIVES OF THE PROJECT ARE TO INVESTIGATE THE RELEASE OF MICROPLASTICS FROM SYNTHETIC TEXTILES, OBSERVE MECHANISTIC BREAKDOWN OF THE RELEASED PLASTICS UNDER ENVIRONMENTALLY RELATED CONDITIONS, AND QUANTIFY NANOSCALE DEGRADATION USING A QUARTZ-CRYSTAL MICROBALANCE.SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES WILL INCLUDE A FINAL REPORT AS WELL AS DOCUMENTING THE FINDINGS OF RELEASE PATTERN OF MICROPLASTICS DURING WASHING, DEGRADATION BEHAVIOR UNDER VISIBLE LIGHT AND HYDROLYSIS IN THE AQUATIC ENVIRONMENT, AND MASS CHANGE DURING DEGRADATION AT THE NANOSCALE LEVEL. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THE PROGRAM INCLUDE STATE AND LOCAL GOVERNMENTS, WASTEWATER TREATMENT PLANT PERSONNEL, HUMAN HEALTH, THE ENVIRONMENT, AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC.
